collect.template: Run python scripts/market_research.py. Report the count of new jobs found. Do not analyze.

scan.description: Analyze collected jobs: requirements, complexity, deliverables, budget
scan.template: Read queue/collected.json. Examine every un-analyzed job. For each: 1) what exactly needs scraping, 2) complexity (low/medium/high), 3) deliverables expected, 4) budget sanity. Assign confidence score (1-5). Save to queue/validated.json with reasoning. Show a ranked summary table.

propos.description: Write a winning Upwork proposal for a validated job
propos.template: Write an Upwork proposal for this job: $ARGUMENTS. Check queue/validated.json first if the job was pre-analyzed. Include: 1) opening that references their exact problem, 2) sample-first strategy — what small sample to run and include, 3) technical approach (2 sentences), 4) clear pricing + timeline, 5) 60-90s Loom video script, 6) one CTA question. Save to jobs/{job_id}/proposal_{job_id}.md. No templates — each proposal must reference the specific job.

scrape.description: Build and test a scraper for a validated job
scrape.template: Build a scraper for: $ARGUMENTS. Check queue/validated.json for prior analysis. Recon the target site with webfetch (tech stack, anti-bot, pagination). Choose tool (BS4 / Playwright / curl_cffi). Write scraper_{job_id}.py. Run it. Fix errors. Deliver output_{job_id}.csv + README_{job_id}.md.

full-cycle.template: Run python scripts/market_research.py. Then read queue/collected.json and scan every job: requirements, complexity, deliverables, budget, confidence scores. Save to validated.json. Show ranked summary.

log-job.template: Log: $ARGUMENTS. Format: job_id | title | budget | connects_spent | status | notes. Append to queue/stats.json. Show totals: won, revenue, avg price, win rate.
